The file '/home/ga/Desktop/gateway_spec.md' is open in a text editor or its contents are displayed in the terminal.
The ConfigMap for the nginx configuration in the 'edge-routing' namespace is shown with 'immutable: true' in YAML or the 'Immutable' checkbox is checked in the Rancher UI.
The 'nginx-gateway' deployment's volume mount for the configuration file shows a 'subPath' (e.g., 'nginx.conf') in the YAML or Rancher UI, ensuring it doesn't obscure the entire directory.
The 'nginx-gateway' deployment shows a volume mount for TLS certificates (likely from a Secret) in the YAML or Rancher UI.

task_completion.description: Resolve CrashLoopBackOff and ensure deployment is running
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'nginx-gateway' deployment in the 'edge-routing' namespace shows an 'Active' state with running pods in the Rancher UI, or '1/1' running status in kubectl output, and is no longer in CrashLoopBackOff.

integrity.description: Agent interacted with the actual Rancher UI or Kubernetes cluster
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen clicking through the Rancher UI in a browser or executing 'kubectl' commands in the terminal, rather than just editing local text files.
The 'Active' or running status of the deployment is shown in the actual Rancher dashboard or standard kubectl output, not a mocked terminal echo or text file.

integrity.description: Configuration changes were actually applied to the cluster
integrity.visual_evidence: The changes to the deployment and ConfigMap are saved and applied in the Rancher UI or via 'kubectl apply', not just written to a local YAML file that is never applied.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task operates on the 'nginx-gateway' deployment in the 'edge-routing' namespace. The specification file is located at '/home/ga/Desktop/gateway_spec.md'.
